data.title: Marqeta to power Klarna Card in the U.S.
data.description:
Card issuing platform Marqeta (NASDAQ:MQ) said on Thursday that it is working with the global digital bank Klarna (KLAR) to power the Klarna Card in the U.S.

Earlier this month, Klarna announced a pilot launch of its new card, which combines debit and pay-over-time features, in collaboration with Visa (V) (VISA:CA).

A broader rollout of the product in the U.S. and Europe is expected later this year.

Marqeta has been powering Klarna's virtual cards in the U.S. since 2018.

data.title: The Baldwin Insurance subsidiary to buy Hippo’s homebuilder distribution network.
data.description:
• The Baldwin Group (NASDAQ:BWIN) subsidiary Westwood Insurance Agency to acquire Hippo’s (NYSE:HIPO) homebuilder distribution network.
  • This acquisition will add eight new homebuilders to Westwood’s client roster, expanding its coverage to 20 of the top 25 homebuilders in the United States, representing over 35% of total new single-family homes built annually
  • Hippo’s homebuilder distribution network generated revenues of approximately $29.2 million in the most recent trailing 12-month period and is expected to deliver approximately $7 million of adjusted EBITDA over the 12 months.

data.title: Cenovus Energy restores full production at Christina Lake oil sands
data.description:
Cenovus Energy (NYSE:CVE) said Thursday it has restarted production at its Christina Lake oil sands facility in Alberta, following a temporary shutdown due to wildfire activity in the area.

Cenovus (NYSE:CVE) said it restarted Christina Lake operations on June 3 and production had ramped up over the course of the week.

The company also said there had been no damages to its infrastructure and it continues to monitor the overall wildfire situation in Alberta.

MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF) said Wednesday that it was ramping up to full operations at its own Christina Lake site following wildfire activity.

data.title: BlackRock sets 2030 aspirations in crypto, private markets, others
data.description:
BlackRock (NYSE:BLK), the largest asset manager in the world, is seeking to become the largest crypto asset manager globally as well as boost its private markets business, the company said Thursday in its Investor Day slides.

The company targets at least $50B crypto assets under management in the next 15 years. It seeks to leverage its experience in the U.S. to expand digital assets exchange-traded products into Europe and Canada.

Private markets form an important part of its overall growth strategy. In the private markets-to-insurance channel, BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) seeks to become the largest third-party manager in balance sheet insurance assets with $700B in assets under management in 2030.

It targets $400B of cumulative fundraising in private markets by 2030.

Its expansion in private markets, digital assets, and active ETFs are intended to drive positive leverage to BlackRock's (BLK) fee rate, said Martin Small, chief financial officer and global head of corporate strategy.

For the company overall, BlackRock (BLK) is targeting doubling its market capitalization to $280B from ~$140B in 2024. It's aiming for a compounded annual growth rate for revenue of 10%, bringing revenue to $35B in 2030 from $20B in 2024, the company said.

It set its adjusted operating income target for $15B vs. $8B achieved in 2024.

BlackRock (BLK) stock slipped 0.6% in Thursday premarket trading.

data.title: Halma plc Non-GAAP EPS of 94.23p, revenue of £2.25B; initiates full-year outlook
data.description:
• Halma plc press release (OTCPK:HLMAF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of 94.23p.
  • Revenue of £2.25B (+10.8% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBIT: +15%, and +13% organic.
  • Strong Adjusted EBIT margin performance: up 80bps at 21.6% (2023/24: 20.8%).
  • Outlook for the 2026 financial year

    "We have made a positive start to the 2026 financial year, with a strong order book and order intake ahead of revenue and last year. While the geopolitical and economic environment remains uncertain, we currently expect to deliver upper single digit percentage organic constant currency revenue growth in this financial year. This includes an expected benefit from further very strong growth in photonics within the Environmental & Analysis Sector. Adjusted EBIT margin is expected to be modestly above the middle of our target range of 19-23%."

data.title: The Lovesac GAAP EPS of -$0.73 beats by $0.06, revenue of $138.4M beats by $0.93M
data.description:
• The Lovesac press release (NASDAQ:LOVE): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.73 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $138.4M (+4.4% Y/Y) beats by $0.93M.
  • Net sales increased $5.8 million, or 4.3%, in the first quarter of fiscal 2026 compared to the prior year period primarily driven by an increase of 2.8% in omni-channel comparable net sales and the net addition of 21 new showrooms. During the first quarter of fiscal 2026, we opened 11 additional showrooms and closed 1 showroom.

  • The Company currently expects the following for the full year of fiscal 2026:

    • Net sales in the range of $700 million to $750 million.
    • Adjusted EBITDA in the range of $48 million to $60 million.
    • Net income in the range of $13 million to $22 million.
    • Diluted income per common share in the range of $0.80 to $1.36 on approximately 16.3 million estimated diluted weighted average shares outstanding.

  • The Company currently expects the following for the second quarter of fiscal 2026:

    • Net sales in the range of $157 million to $166 million.
    • Adjusted EBITDA loss in the range of $2 million to $7 million.
    • Net loss in the range of $8 million to $12 million.
    • Basic loss per common share in the range of $0.58 to $0.83 on approximately 14.6 million estimated weighted average shares outstanding.

data.title: BioNTech to buy German rival CureVac for $1.25B in all-stock deal
data.description:
BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX) has agreed to acquire its German rival CureVac (NASDAQ:CVAC) in an all-stock transaction worth nearly $1.25B, the companies announced Thursday. Shares of CureVac (NASDAQ:CVAC) jumped ~26% in reaction to the announcement, while BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX) ADRs traded flat.

Tübingen, Germany-based CureVac (NASDAQ:CVAC) focuses on developing messenger ribonucleic acid ((mRNA))-based treatments for cancer and infectious diseases, making it a strong fit for BioNTech’s (NASDAQ:BNTX) mRNA-led portfolio, which includes its COVID-19 vaccine developed with Pfizer (PFE).

“This transaction is another building block in BioNTech’s oncology strategy and an investment in the future of cancer medicine,” BNTX’s CEO Ugur Sahin remarked.

“This transaction aims at combining complementary scientific capabilities, proprietary technologies, and manufacturing expertise in the mRNA field under one roof,” added CureVac (CVAC) CEO Alexander Zehnder.

This is a developing story. Check back for more updates.

data.title: TELUS seeks to buy TELUS Digital for $3.40 per share in cash
data.description:
TELUS (NYSE:TU) has submitted a non-binding indication of interest to take full ownership of TELUS International (Cda) Inc. (NYSE:TIXT) (TELUS Digital).

Under the proposed deal, TELUS plans to acquire all issued and outstanding subordinate voting shares and multiple voting shares of TELUS Digital not already owned directly or indirectly by TELUS for a price per share of US$ 3.40 to be paid in cash, TELUS common shares or a combination of both.

The proposed price represents a premium of ~15% to TELUS Digital's closing share price on the NYSE on June 11, 2025.

TELUS has asked the TELUS Digital board of directors to begin a process to review the proposal and appoint a special committee of independent directors to evaluate the proposal.

TELUS currently beneficially owns 152,004,019 multiple voting shares and 6,874,822 subordinate voting shares, representing ~92.5% of the outstanding multiple voting shares, 6.1% of the outstanding subordinate voting shares, representing 57.4% of all outstanding shares, and 86.9% of the combined voting power of all outstanding shares.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Matador Resources announces management shake-up, names William D. Lambert as EVP, CFO, and Head of Strategy
Xtract One Technologies to raise $7M via bought deal offering
data.description:
• Xtract One Technologies (TSX:XTRA:CA) (OTCQX:XTRAF) entered into an agreement with Ventum Capital Markets as underwriter, which agreed to purchase 18M units at a price of $0.39 per unit, for total gross proceeds of $7.02M.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share of the company and one common share purchase warrant.
  • Underwriter an option to purchase up to an additional 15% of the issue.
  • Net proceeds will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.
